revision.text:
[[ja:准位]] [[ja:准位]] [[ja:准位]] [[ja:准位]] '''Jun'i''' (准位) was a standard established under the [[Ritsuryō system]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} to determine the salary allocation for [[Kyūjin]] (宮人), female officials of the imperial court. == Overview == === Kyūjin and Jun'i === For male officials ({{ill|Kanjin|en|Kanjin|qid=Q6363447|lt=Kanjin|1=Kanjin}}), the [[Kan'i sōtō system|official Rank system]] ([[Kan'i sōtō system|官位相当制]]) established a correspondence between [[government posts]] and [[court Ranks]]. However, no such system existed for female officials, the Kyūjin. While some Kyūjin were granted court Ranks through the [[Jojo-i]] (女叙位) system, this did not apply to all Kyūjin. Consequently, the {{ill|Roku-ryō|ja|禄令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roku-ryō|1=Q11591165|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591165}} provided regulations for allocating salaries to Kyūjin based on positions equivalent to specific Ranks, known as "Jun'i." According to the Roku-ryō, "Shokuji" (職事), the so-called "three-tiered officials," were assigned the following Jun'i. For [[Joju]] (女孺), regardless of their governing office, those with Ranks received a salary equivalent to the [[Junior Initial Rank|少初位]] level, while those without a Rank received slightly less (one bolt of cloth deducted). Similar provisions applied to {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}} and {{ill|Ujinome|ja|氏女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujinome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, classified alongside Joju as "Sanji" (散事). {| class="wikitable" |- ! Office/Position !! Shō !! Ten !! Shō |- |rowspan="2"| '''{{ill|Naishi-shi|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-shi|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9404895}}'''
{{small|After {{ill|Daidō 2|ja|大同2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} || Jūgoi || Jūroku-i || Jūshichi-i |- | Jūsan-i || Jūshi-i || Jūgoi |- | '''{{ill|Zō-shi|ja|蔵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zō-shi|1=Q17215452|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17215452}}''' || Shōsan-i || Jūshi-i || Jūgoi |- | '''{{ill|Sho-shi|ja|書司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
'''{{ill|Yaku-shi|ja|薬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
'''{{ill|Den-shi|ja|殿司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-shi|1=Q10369757|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10369757}}''' || Jūroku-i || Jūhachi-i || — |- | '''{{ill|Hei-shi|ja|兵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
'''{{ill|Sen-shi|ja|闡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sen-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' || Shichii || Jūhachi-i || — |- | '''{{ill|Sō-shi|ja|掃司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
'''{{ill|Mizu-shi|ja|水司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' || Jūshichi-i || Jūhachi-i || — |- | '''{{ill|Zen-shi|ja|膳司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zen-shi|1=Q97184776|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97184776}}''' || Shishii || Jūgoi || Shōhachi-i |- | '''{{ill|Sake-shi|ja|酒司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sake-shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' || Rokusii || Jūhachi-i || — |} For titles, positions were named as "Shō," "Ten," and "Shō" corresponding to their offices (e.g., "Naishi-shō," "Naishi-ten," and "Naishi-shō" for the Naishi-shi). Many Shokuji officials were wives and daughters of influential nobles who already held Ranks, leading to inconsistencies in Jun'i regulations. For example, in the 7th year of [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] (714), Sanji with fifth court Rank were equated with Shokuji of the sixth court Rank for salary allocation.''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}},'' Wadō 7, 8th month, day 10. In the 3rd year of {{ill|Jinki|en|Jinki|qid=Q16800180|lt=Jinki|1=Jinki}} (726), fifth-Ranked {{ill|Naimeifu|ja|内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naimeifu|1=Q123696948|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123696948}} serving in positions of sixth Rank or lower were granted salaries equivalent to sixth Rank.''Shoku Nihongi,'' Jinki 3, 2nd month, day pp. In the 4th year of {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} (773), reforms aligned salaries with the higher value of either the official’s personal Rank or the Jun'i of their position.''Shoku Nihongi,'' Hōki 4, 3rd month, day 5. === Rising Status of Naishi-shi and Jun'i === The {{ill|Naishi-shi|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-shi|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9404895}} (officials serving the emperor) initially had a relatively low status. For example, the chief Naishi-shi, {{ill|Naishi-no-kami|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-kami|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11039254}}, held a Jun'i equivalent to Jūgoi, lower than Shōsan-i for Zō-shi or Shishii for Zen-shi. However, influential nobles began appointing their wives as Naishi-no-kami to secure imperial access and gather court intelligence. This practice led to notable appointments such as {{ill|Agata no Inukai no Michiyo|en|Agata no Inukai no Michiyo|qid=Q11582685|lt=Inukami no Michiyo|1=Agata no Inukai no Michiyo}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Ohira|ja|藤原袁比良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Ohira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原宇比良古|1=Fujiwara no Ohirako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623149}}, {{ill|Ōno no Nakayuki|ja|大野仲仟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno no Nakayuki|1=Ōno no Nakachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17222944}}, and {{ill|Abe no Komina|ja|阿倍古美奈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Komina|1=Abe no Komina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17191701}}. To accommodate high-Ranking appointees, reforms adjusted salaries. In 715, Naishi-no-kami holding Jūshi Rank received a salary equivalent to Jūshi.''Shoku Nihongi,'' Jinki 1, 2nd month, day 4. By Hōki 4 (773), higher Ranks superseded Jun'i for salary allocation. Finally, in [[Daidō 2|808]], Naishi-shi Jun'i was elevated: Naishi-no-kami to Jūsan-i, Naishi-no-ten to Jūshi-i, and Naishi-no-shō to Jūgoi.''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}},'' Volume 5. Although Zō-shi officially retained the highest Jun'i, by the early 9th century, Zō-shi had been largely ceremonial, while Naishi-shi became the de facto head of {{ill|Post of the Palace Women|ja|後宮十二司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Post of the Palace Women|1=Twelve Offices of the Back Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10296274}}, solidifying the Naishi-no-kami as the highest-Ranking female official. == References == == Further Reading == * {{ill|Inoue Mitsusada|ja|井上光貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsusada|1=Mitsusada Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372857}}, {{ill|Seki Akira|ja|関晃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Akira|1=Akira Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655819}}, {{ill|Tsuchida Tadachika|ja|土田直鎮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchida Tadachika|1=Naoshige Tsuchida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423817}}, and {{ill|Aoki Kazuo|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Kazuo|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11661670}}, ''Ritsuryō'' (Nihon Shisō Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1994). ISBN 978-4-00-003751-8. * {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|阿部猛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|1=Takeshi Abe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657887}}, ''Nihon Kodai East Asian government position Jiten'' (Kōka Shoten, 1995). ISBN 978-4-00-003751-8. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qid=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system|1=List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles}} {{translated page|ja|准位|version=52195082|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:准位]] {{wikidata link|Q11395384}} [[Category:Imperial Courts in The Nara And Heian Periods]] [[Category:Jun'i]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
